sourceMovie.title: Cruella
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Cruella (2021) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Craig Gillespie's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Deadpool
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Cruella who respond to its comedy will find Deadpool (2016) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Cruella fans keep landing on Moana (2016) — the comedy is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa draws from the same comedy well as Cruella, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2008 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Kick-Ass 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Kick-Ass 2 (2013) captures the crime appeal of Cruella without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts Kick-Ass 2 at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Spies in Disguise
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy appeal of Cruella — that funny edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into Spies in Disguise (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Johnny English Strikes Again
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Johnny English Strikes Again (2018) clearly understood what makes comedy work — the same quality that keeps Cruella rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Lego Movie 2: The Second Part
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lego Movie 2: The Second Part (2019) is the kind of film Cruella fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The comedy overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Abominable
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Abominable shares Cruella's gift for comedy — the 2019 film delivers adventure without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Losers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Cruella compelling — the crime, the adventure — shows up in The Losers (2010) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: An American Tail: Fievel Goes West
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy energy of Cruella is alive in An American Tail: Fievel Goes West (1991), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cruella?
faq.answer: Try Deadpool, Moana, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa—each shares DNA with Cruella while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cruella?
faq.answer: Cruella (2021) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cruella worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
